item.title: US regulators face July 18 deadline to finalize GENIUS Act rules determining which stablecoin issuers may operate in the US market.
Senate CLARITY Act floor vote targeted for week of July 20; bill needs 60 votes and at least seven additional Democratic senators.
US and UK published a 10-point roadmap on July 14 to align oversight of tokenized assets, stablecoins, and digital finance across both markets.
SEC plans to propose a rule in July that would exempt certain crypto activities from securities registration, easing compliance for startups.
Ostium lost an estimated $18-24 million on July 15 after an attacker manipulated oracle price feeds to trigger fraudulent DeFi payouts.
Circle secured final OCC approval on July 10 to charter Circle National Trust, a federally regulated bank for USDC reserve management.
Morgan Stanley won preliminary OCC approval for a digital-asset trust bank to bring crypto custody, staking, and lending in-house.
OCC opened a 60-day public comment period on a proposed AML/CFT supervision framework for stablecoin issuers under the GENIUS Act.
Adjusted stablecoin transaction volume hit a record $1.79 trillion in June 2026, up 63% from May, according to Visa Onchain Analytics.
BlackRock launched BITA, a Bitcoin income ETF that generates yield by selling call options on shares of its IBIT spot Bitcoin fund.
BlackRock's BUIDL tokenized Treasury fund can now be posted as yield-bearing margin collateral on OKX, with Standard Chartered as custodian.
MiCA's EU transition period ended July 1, requiring crypto asset service providers to hold a full licence or cease EU operations.
Taiwan enacted the Virtual Asset Service Act, creating a licensing and regulatory framework for stablecoin issuers and crypto platforms.
A patched Aptos blockchain flaw disclosed in July could have put $70 billion in digital assets at systemic risk; no funds were lost.
